name: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #6
description: Florentine Groteschi #6 is a meme macro primary single-panel relic built around Raphael and Rage Comic Fresco Court. Tarocchi Fiorentini assigns Imperatore d'Occidente, used here as the witness card. A side-quest altar noted in a sacristy inventory gets pulled into an OpenSea trait panel with no fake rarity rank, making the panel read like a corrupted receipt for belief, status, and patronage. The small evidence marks become witnesses, not gimmicks. The generated insert, a saintly captcha angel with broken checkboxes, acts as the modern clue; Gematria marks SIDEQUEST at Σ 714.

attributes.value: a saintly captcha angel with broken checkboxes

florentine_groteschi.collector_note: Custom top-level lore data: not an OpenSea trait list, but intentionally readable in the raw token JSON.

chess_piece.name: Pawn

rarity.ranking_rule: Higher chess strength/rank is intentionally rarer: Pawn common, Knight/Bishop uncommon, Rook rare, Queen epic, King legendary.

board_position.position_rule: Deterministic per-token board square reserved for future game/use mechanics; independent of visible glyph placement.

chess_piece.allocation_rule: Weighted deterministic shuffled chess allocation by piece strength: {'Pawn': 560, 'Knight': 280, 'Bishop': 280, 'Rook': 168, 'Queen': 84, 'King': 29}
chess_piece.metadata_visibility: Hidden from OpenSea attributes; stored only in florentine_groteschi.hidden_traits.
chess_piece.visual_rule: A small unlabeled foreground chess glyph shows the chess-piece graphic with the piece name encoded underneath; that visible code is intentionally not serialized in metadata.

visible_text.title: FLORENTINE GROTESCHI #6
visible_text.main_caption: The wallet connects; the saints request read-only access. The panel opens as a corrupted case file: the Rage Comic Fresco Court layout makes the old scandal look newly clickable; the small evidence marks become witnesses, not gimmicks.
visible_text.history_now_bridge: WORKSHOP BUG REPORT: a side-quest altar noted in a sacristy inventory gets pulled into an OpenSea trait panel with no fake rarity rank. Imperatore d'Occidente reads the jump as patronage theater: who certifies the relic, who pays for belief, and who gets the receipt. The small evidence marks become witnesses, not gimmicks.
visible_text.period_looksmaxx_plaque: RAPHAEL CHERUBCHEEKMAXX MIRROR NOTE: teach the sleeve to frame the wrist inside a Baconian two-ink workshop. The market forgets your pores, kept short for plaque survival.
bottom_provenance_strip: MINCHIATE 97×14+43 // IMPERATORE D'OCCIDENTE // PAL meme macro primary // TRIPLET WITNESS

artwork_breakdown.generated_insert: a saintly captcha angel with broken checkboxes

tarocchi_fiorentini.allocation_rule: 14 complete 97-card sets across 1,401 tokens, plus 43 deterministic extras

artwork_breakdown.composition_read: Raphael anchors the historical source. Imperatore d'Occidente supplies the witness logic. The saintly captcha angel with broken checkboxes acts as the modern meme-object. The meme macro primary palette and Rage Comic Fresco Court styling make the panel read like a corrupted Renaissance case file.

groteschi_explanation.plain_english: Read plainly: Raphael is treated as evidence in a workshop bug report. The old-world clue is a side-quest altar noted in a sacristy inventory; the modern-world echo is an OpenSea trait panel with no fake rarity rank. The joke is that patronage, holy paperwork, and marketplace verification are all status systems asking who gets believed.
groteschi_explanation.read_between_the_lines: The Imperatore d'Occidente card is not decorative; it tells you how to read the room. The old clue and the modern clue compete as evidence, so the panel becomes a custody argument about belief, status, and receipts. The small evidence marks become witnesses, not gimmicks. The slang turns sacred authority into aura, patronage into clout economics, and marginalia into groupchat evidence.

slang_for_normal_plebs.translation_for_normal_plebs: did it perfectly; in this collection a fresco, saint, or relic can 'eat' like a viral post

hidden_solution.method: Split the visible marker into DNA triplets, translate each triplet through the genetic-code table, then read the amino-acid letters as the source Base name. Non-standard letters use documented stop/nearest triplet placeholders.

hidden_acrostic.method: Read the first character of each acrostic line; slashes are spaces.

collector_warnings: If the ledger balances, something has gone terribly wrong.

Opening the metadata counts as participation in the trial.
If the Tarocchi card and QR code disagree, trust the card.
The archive denies all aura liability.
